steps.title: Identify what claim the quote should prove
steps.content: The question asks for a quote that directly shows Aunt Sue's stories come from her personal experiences. We need evidence of the source—where the stories originate, not how they're told or what they contain.

steps.title: Find the quote that makes this explicit
steps.content:
Choice B — a listening child "knows that Aunt Sue / Never got her stories out of any book at all, / But that they came / Right out of her own life." directly quotes the speaker saying that Aunt Sue "Never got her stories out of any book at all, / But that they came / Right out of her own life"—an explicit proof of personal origin.

The other three choices address presentation (imagery in A, setting in C, vocal delivery in D) rather than source.

That matches Choice B — a listening child "knows that Aunt Sue / Never got her stories out of any book at all, / But that they came / Right out of her own life.".
